frequently asked questions f. a. qs new category no — under 1 roof is not a shelter. it is a nonprofit housing organization that provides permanent supportive housing, rapid rehousing, and homelessness prevention services to individuals and families in need. for over 15 years, under 1 roof has served dallas, collin, and denton, rockwall counties, working to keep people housed and help those experiencing homelessness return to stable housing. the organization does this through a comprehensive set of services, including : prevention services – including rental, mortgage, and utility assistance to stop evictions before they happen rapid rehousing – to help individuals and families quickly exit homelessness and stabilize in permanent housing permanent supportive housing ( psh ) – for individuals facing chronic homelessness and high barriers wraparound case management and support services – to promote housing retention, employment, and self - sufficiency under 1 roof focuses on long - term solutions — not temporary shelter — because housing is the foundation for rebuilding lives.

you can also mail a check to : under 1 roof 5787 s hampton rd, suite 390 dallas, tx 75232 every donation helps provide safe, stable housing and critical support services to those in need. thank you for making a difference! under 1 roof benefits from the generosity of an ever growing number of people, businesses, churches and organizations. while the reasons for giving are as varied as their interests and financial circumstances, all share common desire to provide hope, dignity and a brighter future for homeless families receiving our services. at under 1 roof, we believe in transparency, stewardship, and impact. 97 % services | 3 % administration donations to under 1 roof are allocated to various programs and services that directly benefit individuals and families facing homelessness : emergency rental and utility assistance : providing financial support to prevent evictions and utility shut - offs, helping families maintain stable housing. housing navigation and case management : assisting clients in finding and securing housing, and offering personalized support to address their unique needs. essential supplies : supplying basic necessities such as food, cleaning and hygiene products, baby items, and activity packages to support physical and mental well - being. educational support : ensuring children have the tools necessary to meet education requirements, including tablets and ongoing tutoring support. warm and welcoming homes : providing items that make program participants feel they have a home to go to, not just a roof with four walls.

educational support : ensuring children have the tools necessary to meet education requirements, including tablets and ongoing tutoring support. warm and welcoming homes : providing items that make program participants feel they have a home to go to, not just a roof with four walls. under 1 roof emphasizes that every contribution directly fuels their mission to prevent displacement, secure safe housing, and offer the tools people need to rebuild their lives. under 1 roof is a dallas - based nonprofit organization dedicated to providing comprehensive housing and support services to individuals and families experiencing or at risk of homelessness. their programs are designed to promote self - reliance and long - term stability. housing services permanent supportive housing ( psh ) : offers both scattered - site and project - based housing options for homeless families, particularly women and children. clients receive intensive case management and access to essential support services through community partnerships. rapid rehousing : assists individuals and families in quickly exiting homelessness and returning to permanent housing. services include housing search assistance, case management, and referrals to community resources. prevention & stabilization services rental, mortgage, and utility assistance : provides financial support to prevent evictions and utility shut - offs, helping families maintain stable housing. legal education and services : offers quarterly workshops, individual consultations, and legal representation to prevent evictions and address housing - related legal issues. life skills training : provides classes in financial literacy, budgeting, employment readiness and other practical skills to promote self - sufficiency.

quarterly workshops, individual consultations, and legal representation to prevent evictions and address housing - related legal issues. life skills training : provides classes in financial literacy, budgeting, employment readiness and other practical skills to promote self - sufficiency. supportive services professional case management : masters - level social work interns and staff provide individualized case management, crisis resolution counseling, and referrals to community resources. transportation assistance : distributes bus vouchers to clients as needed to facilitate access to employment, education, and services. basic needs support : offers essential items such as food, cleaning and hygiene products, baby items, and activity packages to support physical and mental well - being. under 1 roof serves clients across dallas, collin, and denton counties, focusing on single mothers and children. their holistic approach aims to provide not just housing, but the tools and support necessary for individuals and families to achieve lasting stability. thank you for reaching out to us. we truly appreciate your initiative in seeking support. at this time, all of our housing programs are currently at full capacity and we ’ re not able to offer immediate assistance. that said, we still want to provide information that may help you access support through other avenues or through our agency in the future. to receive housing assistance in dallas county, you ’ ll need to be added to the coordinated access system ( cas ). cas helps track individuals in need of housing assistance and connects them to appropriate resources.

other avenues or through our agency in the future. to receive housing assistance in dallas county, you ’ ll need to be added to the coordinated access system ( cas ). cas helps track individuals in need of housing assistance and connects them to appropriate resources. you can be added to cas through several access points, including emergency shelters, street outreach, or the homeless crisis line. once added to cas, the cas team will assess your situation and provide a referral to an agency that can help connect you to housing. please note that the referral may be to an agency other than ours, depending on your needs and availability. placement in our program will depend on whether we ’ re the right fit for your needs and our current availability. if you are seeking housing in garland, our rapid rehousing program does not require you to be in the coordinated access system. however, you will need to have some connection to garland, such as having lived, worked, or having family there. this program offers housing assistance for up to one year. placement in this program will depend on current availability. please feel free to reach out if you have any questions. we ’ re here to help and hope you find the support you need. resources you may find helpful include : homeless crisis helpline : 888 - 411 - 6802 211 texas : interfaith family services : mlk center : north dallas shared ministries : harmony cdc : home point : resource guide :

the your texas benefits website usually, being a student disqualifies you from receiving snap benefits, even if you meet the other eligibility requirements. however, students who meet specific criteria ( called “ exemptions ” ) may still be eligible for snap. for example, students who work at least 20 hours a week, students with a work - study job, students with a disability that prevents them from working, students who care for a young child and students who are under age 18 or over age 49 may still be eligible for snap. the federal government provides a clear explanation of students ’ eligibility for snap if you are enrolled less than half - time, you are not considered a student for the purposes of snap, which may make it easier for you to receive assistance. visit the office of financial aid to determine if you are enrolled less than half - time. if you are a student who is financially supporting your family, even if you are not eligible for snap, one of your family members might be. for more information about snap eligibility, visit texas health and human services or the usda food and nutrition service. the north texas food bank provides a clear summary of the snap application process nonprofit application assistance for some, the application process for snap can be confusing. several local organizations offer snap application assistance, so you can speak with someone who will explain the application process and help you apply. below are three organizations that offer this service.

text: lost and behind. don ' t end up in the situions i ' m now in. best of luck to you, message me anytime if you need help or guidance. spencer repulsive - land8171 currently living in a cargo van. the key things i ’ ve learned are that you ’ ll need a place to : - shower - laundry - study - cook food / eat food - bathroom the good news is that a lot of campuses offer memberships to local gyms for free. look into that for your showers. laundry at laundromats. consider that in your budgeting ; that can get pricy and changing clothes is key for feeling kind of clean when you ’ re living outside / in a car. you might find yourself going through more laundry than usual. does your campus have a place you can study? a library? coffee shops? you ’ ll have to plan your studying around those hours if so ; late - night studying under flashlight in a car is possible, i ’ ve done it, but not a great time. if you ’ re going to go coffee shop route ( typically more flexible hours ), account for the fact that you ’ ll need to buy a coffee each time you go. food. a lot of campuses offer food or have food banks, but it ’ s usually processed / canned / dried and will need some sort of love.
